context_note: Germany vs Curacao - one of the most lopsided fixtures of the group stage (Curacao is the smallest-population nation ever to qualify for a World Cup, debuting via CONCACAF). Smarkets prices Germany at ~94.6% to win.

smarkets.method: mid-price = avg(best_bid, best_offer) per contract (1/100ths of a percent), vig-removed via normalization across all contracts in a market
no_market_exists_for: team SOT by half or full match

curacao_n3.note: Most recent pre-WC tune-up friendlies vs varied opposition. 4th friendly (vs Aruba) and 4 CONCACAF WCQ matches were in ESPN schedule but returned empty boxscore.statistics - excluded.

q1_curacao_more_fouls_than_germany.method: Skellam normal approx on per-match foul rates (n=8 GER, n=3 CUR): raw P=0.42 (CUR avg fouls 10.0 < GER avg 10.875). BLENDED UP toward 0.48: QAT-SUI Q6 ('Qatar more fouls than Switzerland', a similar huge-underdog-vs-favorite question) had outcome=YES and crowd (0.64) beat our reversed model (0.46) - the underdog-defends-deep-and-fouls-more pattern was real there, and Curacao's n=3 sample (vs Scotland/Australia/China) doesn't capture the game-state of being THIS outclassed.

q2_curacao_offsides_2plus.method: First-pass Poisson, Curacao offsides n=3 (0,3,1), lambda=1.333, P(X>=2)=1-e^-lambda(1+lambda)

q3_germany_more_2h_sot_than_curacao.method: Skellam on 2H-scaled SOT (lambda=full/2): GER 2H lambda=3.625, CUR 2H lambda=1.333. Largest gap of any question - reflects the scale of the SOT differential (7.25 vs 2.67 full-match average).

q4_curacao_more_cards_than_germany.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Most cards' market, vig-adjusted (Curacao 58.82%). Undeviated per RULE2 - our most consistent category (+5.00 avg, 85% correct side over 13 questions).

q5_germany_offsides_2plus.method: First-pass Poisson, Germany offsides n=8, lambda=1.5, P(X>=2)=1-e^-lambda(1+lambda). Same structure and nearly identical value (0.43) to BRA-MAR Q1 (Brazil 2+ offsides vs Morocco's deep defense), which was our biggest win of that match (+20.36) - reinforces using the raw first-pass number unshaded.

q6_germany_win.method: RULE1 (avg(crowd,market)) cannot be computed pre-match (crowd unobservable). Smarkets full-time-result market = 94.59% Germany, vig-adjusted - itself an extreme but genuinely liquid sharp-market price reflecting a real talent gap (Curacao is the smallest-population WC debutant ever). RULE6 (never submit 0%/100%, cap ~90-95%) is naturally satisfied by submitting the market price undeviated - no need for an artificial cap below it. Treating as tier1_market undeviated (our most proven category) rather than a 'gut' number.

q7_total_goals_2_or_fewer.method: Direct from Smarkets Under 2.5 goals market, vig-adjusted (15.91%). Undeviated.

q8_2h_total_goals_2plus.method: Direct from Smarkets 2H Over 1.5 goals market, vig-adjusted (67.43%). Undeviated.

q9_curacao_3plus_sot.method: First-pass Poisson, Curacao SOT n=3, lambda=2.667, raw P(X>=3)=0.498. Shaded DOWN to 0.42: this is a MECHANICAL adjustment (less possession/fewer total touches vs a team that dominates the ball even more than Scotland/Australia/China did), not a vague morale narrative - similar in kind to the USA-PAR Enciso exception (verified, match-specific factor affecting shot SUPPLY).

q10_bacuna_1plus_sot.method: Direct from Smarkets player-SOT market, Leandro Bacuna 1+ SOT offer=22.22% (no bid, illiquid). Submitted close to but slightly below the one-sided offer per usual practice on illiquid quotes.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Curacao be caught offside 2 or more times?
In the second half, will Germany have more shots on target than Curacao?
Will Curacao commit more fouls than Germany?
Will Curacao receive more cards than Germany?
Will Germany be caught offside 2 or more times?
Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?
Will the second half have 2 or more total goals?
Will Curacao have 3 or more shots on target?
questions.note: our only loss this match - crowd was MORE bearish on Curacao than our mechanically-shaded 0.42 (raw 0.498), and crowd was right. In a blowout-favorite scenario, underdog-offense props may need an even larger shade than a single mechanical adjustment captures.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Leandro Bacuna have at least 1 shot on target?

post_match_results.summary: Best match yet by a wide margin: +72.47 RBP, 9/10 beat crowd, all 10 submissions matched the researched recommendation exactly (zero gut overrides). Germany won 7-1, an even MORE dominant result than the already-lopsided 94.6% market price implied. Every 'Curacao does X' question resolved NO and every 'Germany dominates / high-scoring' question resolved YES - exactly the direction both the verified Smarkets market and our tier2_realdata first-pass models leaned. Crowd was systematically more bullish on Curacao than us on 5/6 props (Q1,Q3,Q4,Q5,Q10) and lost on all of them; the lone exception (Q9) is where crowd was MORE bearish on Curacao than us and crowd won. Full postmortem: MATCH_LOG.md 'GER vs CUR postmortem' section.
